objective: Layer artifact-kind/schema registries, adaptive agent-directed intake, Charter candidate/promotion authority, a resolved posture kernel, and a research/user-decision gate for shipped defaults through the Handbook Contract Membrane control pack without changing Rust.

sections: Phase 1 — Profile and artifact semantic kernel
Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections
Intake record and artifact candidate contracts
Project posture kernel and recommendation contracts

work_completed: Separated reusable ArtifactKindDefinition contracts from profile-selected ArtifactInstanceDescriptor state.
Defined safe repository-local custom schemas, meta-validation, generic operations, and the no-Rust-enum/no-dynamic-command boundary.
Converted the historical Charter questionnaire concept into a versioned CharterIntakeDefinition coverage contract used by a skill-directed external LLM agent.
Defined guided-adaptive, express, and agent-assisted acquisition modes that converge on one candidate schema and expose provenance, unknowns, contradictions, and approvals.
Separated immutable intake records, non-canonical artifact candidates, approved canonical Charter YAML, and derived Markdown/Resolution projections.
Defined ProjectPostureKernel, PostureEvaluationPolicy, PostureRecommendation, and authorized PostureTransition semantics tied to Snapshot Memory and hysteresis.
Reserved the shipped default artifact set for HCM-0.6 research plus a user brainstorming/decision session rather than inferring defaults from existing code or examples.
Added semantic references to handoff and dispatch infrastructure while preserving old immutable handoff compatibility.

deliverables.summary: Added kind/instance, intake/Charter, posture, skill/CLI/Substrate ownership, default-set research, and non-negotiable boundaries.
Defined artifact kinds/instances, custom schemas, intake coverage and acquisition modes, Charter authority, posture resolution, recommendations, transitions, and snapshot integration.
Recorded current precursors and target gaps for kind registries, shipped defaults, Charter intake, posture resolution, and skill behavior.
Added HCM-0.6 default-set research/decision, kind/schema and instance slices, Charter/custom-intake proofs, and the posture recommendation loop.
Added preliminary kind, instance, intake, candidate/promotion, Charter, validation-layer, posture, threshold, notification, and stable generic operation contracts.
Added current Charter coverage precursor plus default, kind, intake, Charter, and posture proof gates and regression rules.
Added backward-compatible optional semantic refs for artifact, intake, canonical, posture, and default-set decision records.

decisions.summary: Artifact kinds are reusable versioned schema/intake/validation/projection definitions; artifact instances bind kinds to repository paths, labels, requiredness, roles, and dependencies.
decisions.authority_effect: Profiles select instances without turning every custom artifact into a hard-coded product type.

decisions.summary: Repository-defined custom kinds use safely resolved local schemas and stable generic CLI/SDK operations without Rust enum variants, executable schema hooks, remote fetching, or dynamic commands.
decisions.authority_effect: Customizability remains data-driven and transport command semantics stay stable.

decisions.summary: The historical Charter questionnaire becomes CharterIntakeDefinition; a skill-directed external LLM agent conducts guided-adaptive, express, or agent-assisted acquisition against one coverage/candidate schema.
decisions.authority_effect: Handbook owns deterministic coverage/evaluation/promotion without restoring a nested CLI wizard or hidden model call.

decisions.summary: Intake records are descriptive provenance, artifact candidates are proposals, canonical Charter YAML is constitutional authority, and Markdown/GUI/packet/agent views are projections.
decisions.authority_effect: Collected intake remains auditable without creating a second editable Charter authority.

decisions.summary: ProjectPostureKernel is a deterministic resolved view; policy-controlled snapshot/evidence triggers emit advisory PostureRecommendation records, and only authorized PostureTransition records change canonical policy.
decisions.authority_effect: Dynamic guidance gains thresholds, hysteresis, notifications, and auditability without self-mutating governance.

decisions.summary: The shipped default artifact set remains unresolved until HCM-0.6 completes focused research and a user brainstorming/decision session.
decisions.authority_effect: Existing enums, historical artifacts, templates, and illustrative examples cannot become defaults by inertia.

findings.summary: Artifact-kind/schema, adaptive intake, canonical promotion, and posture contracts are target-only; their new proof gates remain open until bounded implementation slices land.
The future shipped default artifact set requires dedicated research and user product/design authority; it must not be selected during implementation.
HCM-0.1 still requires a fresh independent review of the complete expanded control pack before implementation authority can freeze.

escalations.trigger: The shipped default artifact set would otherwise be inferred from current implementation or illustrative candidate names.
escalations.target_resolution: HCM-0.6 research and user product/design decision
escalations.decision_required: Approve the versioned shipped artifact kinds, default instances, and requiredness posture after comparing evidence-backed candidate sets.
options: Fuller optional-by-default catalog with a smaller required core
escalations.recommendation: Run focused research first, then a user brainstorming session that evaluates minimal, standard, and fuller candidates before recording the approved default.
escalations.resume_condition: Phase 1 shipped-default implementation may begin only after HCM-0.6 records the approved kind/instance/requiredness set.

validation.command: Python Draft 2020-12 schema validation for handoff schema/template/all records and ledger entries
Control-pack Markdown link, semantic-threading, portability, and phase-ID assertions
git diff --check and docs-only changed-path assertion

pack_updates.reason: Make kind/schema, intake/Charter, posture, semantic refs, and unresolved shipped defaults part of the program control surface.
Keep the concise lineage aligned while leaving implementation-grade detail in the control pack.
Prevent orchestration from conflating semantic layers, inferring shipped defaults, or accepting dynamic commands/hidden intake synthesis.
Carry exact semantic refs and reserved user decisions through durable transitions.

next_session.objective: Select this superseding handoff and dispatch a fresh independent docs-only review of the complete HCM-0.1 control pack, including artifact kind/instance separation, custom-schema safety, adaptive Charter intake, posture authority, Snapshot integration, default-set reservation, sequencing, and proof gates.

sections: Phase 1 — Profile and artifact semantic kernel
Phase 3 — Vocabulary, Context Resolution, Snapshot Memory, and projections
Project posture kernel and recommendation contracts

prohibited_scope: Selecting the shipped default artifact set without the HCM-0.6 research/user decision
Restoring a nested or question-by-question CLI wizard
acceptance_criteria: A fresh review checks kind/instance/schema ownership, custom-kind safety, intake authority/provenance, one-schema acquisition modes, Charter promotion/projection, posture thresholds/hysteresis/notifications, and Snapshot integration.
The review verifies that candidate artifact names are not treated as an approved shipped default set and that HCM-0.6 requires research plus user decision.
Cross-document terminology, phase sequencing, schemas, semantic refs, orchestration, and proof gates are cohesive and concrete.
Actionable findings are remediated through a bounded dispatch and durable superseding handoff.
HCM-0.1 closes only after the complete control-pack proof gate is satisfied.
stop_conditions: Stop and record an escalation if review exposes an unresolved authority boundary among intake, canonical artifacts, posture, snapshots, or contracts.
Do not select the shipped default set during review; route that decision to HCM-0.6.
Do not begin Rust implementation before HCM-0.1 review closes and a slice-local packet is approved.

chat_summary: Layered artifact kinds/instances, custom schemas, adaptive Charter intake, canonical promotion, resolved posture recommendations, semantic handoff refs, and the HCM-0.6 default-set research/user-decision gate through the control pack. HCM-0.1 remains review-required; no Rust changed.
